info: MSCSLM (Mathematically Similarity Checked Small Language Model) is a JavaScript-based open-source project focused on research in Mathematically Similarity Checked Small Language Models. It comprises three key components: Data, Similarity Algorithm, and Input/Output functionalities. The project's workflow involves preprocessing user input, checking similarity against a dataset, and presenting the top 2 results with the highest similarity scores. Features include a chatbot, search engine, spelling correction, and synonym handling. Future improvements aim to enhance algorithms, incorporate Natural Language Understanding (NLU), support multiple languages, enable dynamic responses, and integrate user feedback mechanisms. Contributions are welcome under the MIT License. To use MSCSLM, clone the repository, add queries and responses in response.json, and integrate it into your website or application.

hello: Hello! 👋 I'm Sajilo Sathi, your guide to child safety, support services, and local resources in Nepal. How can I help you today? You can ask me about child abuse, scams, mental health, emergency contacts, and more.
hi: Hello! 👋 I'm Sajilo Sathi, your guide to child safety, support services, and local resources in Nepal. How can I help you today? You can ask me about child abuse, scams, mental health, emergency contacts, and more.
hey: Hello! 👋 I'm Sajilo Sathi, your guide to child safety, support services, and local resources in Nepal. How can I help you today? You can ask me about child abuse, scams, mental health, emergency contacts, and more.
how are you: I'm running great, thank you for asking! 😊 I'm here to help you find the right resources and support. What can I assist you with today?
what can you do: I can help you with: 📋 Child abuse reporting (city-wise), 🚨 Emergency contacts in Nepal, 💰 Scam reporting and prevention, 🧠 Mental health support resources, 👮 Police, fire, and ambulance contacts, 🏠 Domestic violence helplines, and more. Just ask me anything!
help: Sure, I'm here to help! You can ask me about: child abuse, scams, mental health, emergency contacts, domestic violence, human trafficking, elder abuse, or any support services in Nepal. Try asking 'child abuse Kathmandu' or 'scam report'.
thank you: You're welcome! 😊 Stay safe, and don't hesitate to reach out if you need anything else. Remember: in an emergency, always dial 100 for police.
thanks: You're welcome! 😊 Stay safe, and don't hesitate to reach out if you need anything else. Remember: in an emergency, always dial 100 for police.
bye: Take care! 👋 Remember, help is always just a message or call away. Stay safe!
goodbye: Goodbye! Take care and stay safe. 👋 If you ever need help again, I'm here.
scam: 🚨 Scam Reporting in Nepal: To report a scam, contact the Cyber Bureau of Nepal Police at +977-1-4412053 or email cyberbureau@nepalpolice.gov.np. For online fraud, visit: https://cybercrime.gov.np. Tips: Never share OTP or bank details over the phone. Verify caller identity before sharing personal information. Report suspicious activity immediately.
scam report: 🚨 To report a scam in Nepal: 1) Cyber Bureau Nepal Police: +977-1-4412053 | Email: cyberbureau@nepalpolice.gov.np | Website: cybercrime.gov.np. 2) Nepal Police: Dial 100. 3) Nepal Telecommunications Authority (NTA): 9600 for telecom fraud. Keep evidence like screenshots, call logs, and transaction records.
online scam: 🚨 Online Scam Help: Report to Cyber Bureau Nepal Police: +977-1-4412053 | cyberbureau@nepalpolice.gov.np | cybercrime.gov.np. Common online scams in Nepal: fake job offers, lottery scams, romance scams, phone call fraud. Never share: OTP codes, passwords, bank account details, or national ID to unknown callers.
phone scam: 📱 Phone/Call Scam Prevention: Never share OTP or bank PINs over the phone. Banks never call asking for passwords. If someone claims to be from your bank, hang up and call your bank's official number directly. Report scam calls to: Nepal Police (100) or NTA (9600).
cyber crime: 💻 Cybercrime Reporting: Cyber Bureau of Nepal Police — Phone: +977-1-4412053 | Email: cyberbureau@nepalpolice.gov.np | Website: cybercrime.gov.np | Address: Naxal, Kathmandu. You can also file a complaint at your nearest District Police Office.
cybercrime: 💻 Cybercrime Reporting: Cyber Bureau of Nepal Police — Phone: +977-1-4412053 | Email: cyberbureau@nepalpolice.gov.np | Website: cybercrime.gov.np | Address: Naxal, Kathmandu. You can also file a complaint at your nearest District Police Office.

anxiety: 💙 For anxiety and stress support in Nepal: Government Mental Health Helpline: 1166. TPO Nepal: 01-4468190. CMC Nepal: 01-4168830. Breathing exercises, talking to trusted people, and professional counseling can all help. You don't have to face it alone.

child helpline: 📞 Child Helpline Nepal: Call 1098 — This is a free, 24/7 helpline for children in need of help, protection, or support. Operated by Child Workers in Nepal (CWIN). Also contact: Central Child Welfare Board: 01-4243558.
child abuse: 🚨 Child Abuse Reporting in Nepal: National Child Helpline: 1098 (free, 24/7). Police: 100. Central Child Welfare Board: 01-4243558. Women, Children & Senior Citizen Service Directorate: 01-4412950. Please specify your city for ward-level contact information (e.g. 'child abuse Kathmandu').
